title: Vanilla Custard Mochi
description: A Japanese-inspired dessert featuring a rich vanilla custard filling enclosed in a soft and chewy mochi made with argentinian rice flour.
instructions: Combine rice flour and water in a bowl to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 4-6 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Heat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat.
Cook each mochi disk for 2-3 minutes on each side, until it's lightly browned and puffed up.
Meanwhile, prepare the custard filling by mixing the rich custard with vanilla extract.
Assemble the mochi by placing a spoonful of custard filling in the center of each mochi disk.
Fold the mochi over the filling and press the edges together to seal.
Serve the vanilla custard mochi chilled or at room temperature.
